name: Bol d'Or Mirabaud

course.summary: Mass start off Genève-Plage, east up the whole lake to round the Barge du Bouveret, then back to finish at the SNG. Longest closed-circuit inland race in the world.

start.name: Genève-Plage / SNG
start.note: Monohull line ~1500 m in 4 sections; leave the port-end mark and shore to port. LJ starts on the monohull line.

rounding.name: Barge du Bouveret
rounding.note: Monohull entry buoy (mark 8) 46.398/6.851 then round the barge (mark 9). Sunday 08:00 the barge may become an orange buoy.

finish.name: SNG jetty, Genève
finish.note: Entry mark (10) 46.218/6.169, then finish at the SNG jetty. Leave the safety buoy to port.

course.note: Route waypoints trace the lake's crescent (bulges north through the Grand Lac); the return retraces it. Not the sailed track — that's on the Debrief/Rivals tabs.

wind_climatology.summary: Léman is thermally driven and shore-shadowed. Mornings are often light/variable; gradient and thermal breezes build through the day. The classic Bol d'Or is a light-air drifter punctuated by local systems — 2026 had 81% of the race below 6 kt.

patterns.name: Bise
patterns.when: cold, dry high-pressure NE; any time, often strong
patterns.character: Steady, can be 15–30 kt; kicks up chop in the Petit Lac. Gives an upwind exit from Geneva and a downwind return.
patterns.tactics: Bise favours the SOUTH (French/Savoie) bank upwind — stronger pressure mid-lake, the most reliable Léman pattern. Fast race, downwind return is the passing lane.

patterns.name: Vent (SW)
patterns.when: prevailing gradient + afternoon thermal on warm days
patterns.character: Builds midday; fills the Petit Lac and Grand Lac from the SW. The workhorse breeze.
patterns.tactics: Afternoon filling from the SW favours the boats that got east first; watch for it filling from behind on the outbound leg.

patterns.name: Vaudaire
patterns.dir: SE föhn from the Rhône valley
patterns.when: föhn conditions; strongest in the Haut Lac near Bouveret
patterns.character: Warm, gusty, can be violent at the east end — a different world from the Petit Lac.
patterns.tactics: Decisive at the Bouveret rounding: the east end can have breeze while the middle is glassy. Approach with a plan for a big shift/pressure change.

patterns.name: Joran / evening thermals
patterns.dir: NW off the Jura (Joran); local Molard/Rebat/Séchard
patterns.character: Descends on the north (Vaud) shore in the evening; local thermals off both shores.
patterns.tactics: Overnight and evening legs reward the crew who reads the shore thermals rather than sitting in the dying gradient.

tactical_zones.name: Petit Lac (Geneva → Yvoire/Nyon)
tactical_zones.knowledge: Narrow, shifty, shore-shadowed. In light air hug the shore that has pressure — Vaud (Swiss, N) vs Savoie (French, S). Big fleet = dirty air; a clean lane matters more than the shortest line.

tactical_zones.name: Grand Lac (widest section)
tactical_zones.knowledge: More gradient-driven and open. The 2026 lesson: commit to the Savoie (French, S) shore early on the return rather than beating back down the middle — that's where LJ lost ground.

tactical_zones.name: Haut Lac / Bouveret (the rounding)
tactical_zones.knowledge: Mountain-influenced; Vaudaire risk. Wind and pressure can differ sharply from the middle of the lake. Plan the rounding approach for a likely shift and a possible park-up.

lake_model: Alplakes (waves/current only — NEVER a wind source)

tracking.note: SuiviRegate KMZ is the GPS source we replay in Rivals; manage2sail carries official SRS/TCF results.

editions.conditions: Extraordinarily light — AROME wind on LJ's track shows 81% of the race below the 6 kt polar floor; a morning drifter with a weak afternoon SW.
editions.note: LJ mid-fleet in TCF3; lost ground on the Grand Lac return by not committing to the French shore early (see Debrief).

regatta_intelligence.note: Deeper per-boat analysis (top rivals, routes, shore split) is loaded on the Rivals and Debrief tabs. Add prior editions as their GPS/results are ingested.

_reuse: shoreline, stations & Bise insight reused from the Bol d'Or tactical app (JPSMONT/bol-dor-tactical).
